title: rubus moluccanus var. elongatus
intro: Rubus moluccanus var. elongatus, also known as the Moluccan raspberry or Elongated Moluccan raspberry, is a variant of the Rubus moluccanus plant, a species of bramble that is native to Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its high content of anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rubus moluccanus var. elongatus include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. In herbal medicine, the plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies for its purported ability to treat various health conditions.
